rationale: Best continuation of the v15 style: teal museum/natural-history lighting, premium macro look, calm shark subject.
Visually distinct enough from Shot 1: reads like a museum/specimen proof beat rather than open ancient ocean.
Clean mobile-readable composition with one main shark and a stone/slab base.
No generated text, no obvious trees/plants/grass, and fewer distracting prop artifacts than v17.
caveat: It is more of a museum/specimen evidence beat than a clear fossil proof. v17 tried fossil/tooth proof but drifted into aquarium/display-case clutter and coral/plant-like shapes.
runner_up: v16 variant 05 — very clean museum case and matches style, but shark is smaller and less immediate.
rejected_lessons: Prompting fossil/tooth too hard caused display case/terrarium compositions and coral-like props.
For this short, Shot 2 can function as the dry “Seriously” pause if it feels premium and calm; save literal dates/proof for timeline shot.
next_if_refining: One micro-pass could combine v16_03 cleanliness with a single subtle fossil slab texture, but avoid words that trigger aquarium, coral, glass box, or display case.
